---
title: "R Package"
toc: true
---

The `repos` R package wraps the same Bash scripts used by the command-line
tool, so every feature available at the terminal is also available from R.

## Installation

```r
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("MiguelRodo/repos")
```

**System requirements:** `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq` must be available on
your `PATH`.

## End-to-End Workflow

### Step 1 — Create a `repos.list` file

Place a `repos.list` file in your project directory listing the repositories
you want to manage (one per line):

```
myorg/data-curation
myorg/analysis
myorg/documentation
```

See the [repos.list reference](repos-list.html) for the full syntax — branch
pinning, worktrees, visibility flags, and more.

### Step 2 — Clone and configure repositories

```r
library(repos)

repos_setup()
```

This reads `repos.list`, creates any missing GitHub repositories, clones
everything to the **parent directory** of your current location, and generates a
VS Code workspace file.  See [`repos_setup()`](#repos_setup) below for all options.

### Step 3 — Run a pipeline

Once the repositories are cloned, run a script inside each one:

```r
repos_run()          # executes run.sh in every repo
```

See [`repos_run()`](#repos_run) below for all options.

## API Reference

### `repos()` {#repos}

Top-level dispatcher that delegates to `repos_setup()` or `repos_run()`.

```r
repos(command, ...)
```

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `command` | `character` | `"setup"` or `"run"` |
| `...` | | Forwarded to `repos_setup()` or `repos_run()` |

**Returns:** Invisibly returns the exit status of the script (0 for success).

**Examples:**

```r
library(repos)

repos("setup")
repos("setup", public = TRUE)
repos("run")
repos("run", script = "build.sh")
```

---

### `repos_setup()` {#repos_setup}

Clone and configure repositories from a `repos.list` file.

```r
repos_setup(
  file        = NULL,   # path to a custom list file (default: repos.list)
  public      = FALSE,  # create new repos as public
  codespaces  = FALSE,  # configure Codespaces authentication
  devcontainer = NULL,  # path(s) to devcontainer.json (implies codespaces = TRUE)
  permissions = NULL,   # "all" or "contents" — passed to codespaces-auth-add.sh
  tool        = NULL,   # force tool for codespaces-auth-add.sh ("jq" or "python")
  debug       = FALSE,  # write debug output to stderr
  debug_file  = NULL,   # write debug output to a file (TRUE = auto-name, or a path)
  ...                   # additional raw flags forwarded to setup-repos.sh
)
```

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | `character` | `NULL` | Path to repos list file (default: `repos.list`) |
| `public` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Create new repositories as public |
| `codespaces` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Enable Codespaces authentication |
| `devcontainer` | `character` or `character vector` | `NULL` | Path(s) to `devcontainer.json` |
| `permissions` | `character` | `NULL` | `"all"` or `"contents"` |
| `tool` | `character` | `NULL` | Force `"jq"` or `"python"` for auth helper |
| `debug` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Print debug output to stderr |
| `debug_file` | `logical` or `character` | `NULL` | Write debug output to a file |
| `...` | | | Additional raw flags forwarded as-is |

**Returns:** Invisibly returns the exit status of the script (0 for success).

**Examples:**

```r
library(repos)

# Default — reads repos.list in the current directory
repos_setup()

# Use a custom list file
repos_setup(file = "my-repos.list")

# Create new repositories as public
repos_setup(public = TRUE)

# Enable Codespaces auth
repos_setup(codespaces = TRUE)

# Specify devcontainer paths (also enables Codespaces auth)
repos_setup(devcontainer = ".devcontainer/devcontainer.json")

# Combine options
repos_setup(public = TRUE, codespaces = TRUE, debug = TRUE)

# Backward compatibility — raw flag passing still works
repos_setup("--public", "--codespaces")
```

For the full behavioural description, see the [repos setup guide](setup.html).

---

### `repos_run()` {#repos_run}

Execute a script inside each cloned repository.

```r
repos_run(
  file             = NULL,   # path to a custom list file (default: repos.list)
  script           = NULL,   # script to run in each repo (default: run.sh)
  include          = NULL,   # repo name(s) to include
  exclude          = NULL,   # repo name(s) to skip
  ensure_setup     = FALSE,  # run repos_setup() before executing scripts
  skip_deps        = FALSE,  # skip install-r-deps.sh
  dry_run          = FALSE,  # print what would run without executing
  verbose          = FALSE,  # verbose logging
  continue_on_error = FALSE, # keep running after a failure
  ...                        # additional raw flags forwarded to run-pipeline.sh
)
```

**Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file` | `character` | `NULL` | Path to repos list file (default: `repos.list`) |
| `script` | `character` | `NULL` | Script file to run in each repo (default: `run.sh`) |
| `include` | `character` or `character vector` | `NULL` | Only run in these repos |
| `exclude` | `character` or `character vector` | `NULL` | Skip these repos |
| `ensure_setup` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Run `repos_setup()` first |
| `skip_deps` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Skip R dependency installation |
| `dry_run` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Preview without executing |
| `verbose` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Verbose logging |
| `continue_on_error` | `logical` | `FALSE` | Continue past failures |
| `...` | | | Additional raw flags forwarded as-is |

**Returns:** Invisibly returns the exit status of the script (0 for success).

**Examples:**

```r
library(repos)

# Run the default script (run.sh) in every repo
repos_run()

# Run a custom script
repos_run(script = "build.sh")

# Only run in specific repos
repos_run(include = c("backend", "frontend"))

# Skip a repo
repos_run(exclude = "docs")

# Continue even if one repo fails
repos_run(continue_on_error = TRUE)

# Preview without running
repos_run(dry_run = TRUE)

# Ensure repos are cloned before running
repos_run(ensure_setup = TRUE)

# Combine options
repos_run(script = "test.sh", verbose = TRUE, ensure_setup = TRUE)

# Backward compatibility — raw flag passing still works
repos_run("--script", "build.sh", "--dry-run")
```

For the full behavioural description, see [Running Pipelines](pipelines.html).

## GitHub Authentication

`repos_setup()` needs a GitHub token to create repositories and clone private
ones.  Provide it via the `GH_TOKEN` environment variable or via the `gh` CLI:

```r
Sys.setenv(GH_TOKEN = "your_personal_access_token")
repos_setup()
```

Or set it in your shell before starting R:

```bash
export GH_TOKEN="your_personal_access_token"
```
